text: Welcome to ApexReach B2B Lead Engine. This is a complete voiced walkthrough of every feature on the platform.
The sidebar shows our brand identity, Google sign-in integration, navigation tabs, and live connection status indicators.
The Console dashboard displays four key performance indicators: Total Leads Ingested, Ready for Proposal, Outreach Sent, and Highly Rated Leads.
Below the stats, you can see the Pipeline Execution Log showing real-time sync activity, and the Launch Outreach Campaign panel.
Clicking Configure and Launch takes us directly to the CRM tab with all pending leads pre-selected.
This is the Leads CRM directory. It displays all scraped businesses with their name, category, Google rating, area, email, and lifecycle stage.
We can search leads by name, category, or phone number. Filtering for Luxe Couture.
We can filter leads by lifecycle stage. Showing only NEW uncontacted leads.
Now showing CONTACTED leads that have already received outreach.
Clicking a lead row opens the Proposal Outreach Preview panel on the right, showing the personalized message template with dynamic variables.
We can select all leads using the header checkbox. The outreach send button appears with the count of selected leads.

The custom message override lets you write a completely personalized outreach message instead of using the default template. You can insert dynamic tags like lead name, preview URL, rating, and signature.
Each lead has a View Web Page button that opens their auto-generated AI landing page in a new tab.
The Maps Scraper tab is the lead acquisition engine. ApexReach supports ten different scraper providers.
Google Places API with free tier and high quality data
Google Maps Free Web Scraper using Playwright headless browser
DuckDuckGo Search Scraper with HTML crawling, completely free
OpenStreetMap Overpass API, one hundred percent free with no API key required
Jiji dot N G Crawler using Playwright headless browser, completely free
Apify Google Maps Actor for high scale paid scraping
Instagram Scraper for finding e-commerce businesses
Facebook Scraper for shop pages and marketplace listings
TikTok Scraper for product video creators
LinkedIn Scraper for professional service providers
Lets execute a Google Places scrape for Car Dealers in Lagos with a limit of 5 results.
The Verification Rules panel explains the automated quality checks: website filtering, outreach sync to Google Sheets, and rating floor thresholds.
The Sync Logs tab shows the complete system audit trail. Every scrape, outreach dispatch, and pipeline event is timestamped and logged with its status.
Clicking Refresh Timeline pulls the latest log entries from the database.
The Settings tab contains the complete system configuration. Lets walk through every section.
Section one, Global Configuration. Here you set the Storage Backend Mode, which supports Hybrid, Local JSON, Supabase Postgres, and Google Sheets only modes.
The Default Outreach Channel selector supports Email, WhatsApp, Twilio Cold Call, Jiji Chat, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, and LinkedIn outreach.
You also configure the Business Signature, Google Spreadsheet ID, Google Places API Key, and Gemini AI API Key for copywriting.
Section two, Email Outreach Provider. Switch between Google Workspace Gmail OAuth, Resend dot com API, and Brevo dot com SMTP.
Gmail OAuth shows fields for Client ID, Client Secret, and Project ID.
Resend shows the API Key and verified From Email fields.
Brevo shows the API Key, Sender Display Name, and Sender Verified Email fields.
Section three, WhatsApp Outreach Provider, with an enable toggle checkbox.
Meta Cloud API shows Phone Number ID, Access Token, Template Name, and Language Code.
Evolution API shows the Base URL, Instance API key, Instance Name, and a custom text message template.
Whapi dot cloud shows the bearer token field and a custom message template.
Section four, Twilio Voice Cold Calling. Configure the Account SID, Auth Token, From Phone Number, and Call Message Template for automated AI voice calls.
Section five, Jiji Bulk Messaging Outreach with login credentials and a customizable message template using dynamic placeholders.

Below that, you can customize message templates for Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, and LinkedIn outreach channels. Each template supports lead name, rating, review count, preview URL, and signature placeholders.
Finally, the Dry Run Simulation Mode toggle. When enabled, no real API calls are made. This is essential for testing your pipeline safely.
Click Save Configuration Settings to persist all changes to the server.

Back in the sidebar, the Google Cloud Integration panel lets you enter a Project ID and initiate OAuth sign-in. Once linked, your Google identity is displayed with a green status indicator and a sign-out button.
The Sync Pipeline button in the header refreshes all stats, leads, and logs simultaneously.
This is the auto-generated AI-powered landing page for a scraped lead. Each business gets a unique, conversion-focused website.
The sticky preview banner at the top shows a personalized message and a Claim Website and Domain call-to-action button.
The hero section features a dynamic background image, star rating badge, AI-generated headline and subtitle, and call-to-action buttons.
The social proof reputation bar displays the Google rating, verified review count, and locally verified service badge.
The services grid shows AI-generated specialties with emoji icons, titles, and descriptions tailored to the business category.
The About section includes the business address, phone number, an AI-generated description, and a workspace image with a reputation overlay card.
Customer testimonials are AI-generated based on the business category, with star ratings and reviewer names.
The Interactive Automation Demo section lets visitors test-drive the booking workflow. Lets fill in a test submission.
The automation fires instantly, sending a notification to the business owner with all the booking details.
Finally, the Claim This Website and Domain section is the conversion goal. The business owner enters their name and email to request ownership of the auto-generated site.
That concludes the full ApexReach walkthrough. We covered every feature: dashboard, CRM, all ten scrapers, logs, every settings option, and AI preview pages. Thank you for watching!
